preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes: v1.22.2: First-class 9:16 (vertical Stories/Reels/TikTok/Shorts) support in the image pipeline. Pre-fix, imageFormat="9:16" silently coerced to "both" (4:5 + 1:1) because neither the primary-aspect map nor the formats switch in main.go recognized the token — the brief told fal.ai "vertical 4:5" while we asked for 9:16, AND we returned Feed-default pair instead of the 1080x1920 vertical the caller wanted. Live user report 2026-05-11: "imageFormat: 9:16 silently coerces to both (4:5 + 1:1)". Fix introduces a single canonicalImageFormat alias-fold switch that runs BEFORE both the primary-aspect map and the formats switch. Tokens (case-insensitive, whitespace-trimmed): portrait/4:5 → portrait (1080x1350 Feed); square/1:1 → square (1080x1080 universal); story/9:16/vertical/reel/reels → story (1080x1920 Stories/Reels/TikTok/Shorts); landscape/16:9 → landscape (1920x1080 YouTube/web hero); both (default) → portrait+square; all → portrait+square+story (full ad-platform spread in one generation). Unknown tokens log a [WARN] line naming the rejected token and listing valid options, then fall back to "both" — silent coercion was the bug. Canonical token threads through BOTH the primary-aspect map (so the brief tells fal.ai the right ratio) AND the formats switch (so the output matches) — adding a new alias requires only touching one switch. Locked with autocmo-core/image_format_test.go (6 source-scan tests covering: alias coverage, 9:16 sets primaryAspect=9:16, story emits single 9:16 spec, all emits 3 specs including 9:16, unknown token warns + falls back, case-insensitive folding). MCP tool schema autoCMO/app/mcp-tools.js imageFormat describe() updated to list every valid token. Hard-Won learning: every alias-fold switch that drives multiple downstream behaviors must be canonicalized ONCE upstream — pre-fix this code had two switches in two different blocks of main.go that disagreed on what tokens existed, which is how the silent-coerce bug was structurally possible.
whatsNew: Image generation now supports 9:16 vertical (Stories / Reels / TikTok / Shorts) as a first-class format
imageFormat accepts: portrait/4:5, square/1:1, story/9:16/vertical, landscape/16:9, both (default), all
Unknown imageFormat tokens log a warning instead of silently coercing to the default
